pending.body: `src/cli/run.rs:1536-1599` now treats a live pid-only legacy run as `Stale` unless a fresh writer lease also exists. That flows into automatic stale recovery in `src/cli/run.rs:1874-1919` and `src/cli/run.rs:3042-3070`, so `run status` can report "stale" and `run resume`/`run stop` can rewrite the snapshot even while a mixed-version orchestrator is still alive. Recovery should only happen when the orchestrator PID is actually dead or missing; otherwise this change can leave two orchestrators acting on the same project.

`active-processes.json` updates are not concurrency-safe. `ProcessBackendAdapter` now registers/removes child pids around each spawn (`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1160-1169`, `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1255-1256`), but the filesystem helpers do unsynchronized read-modify-write cycles (`src/adapters/fs.rs:456-483`). Final-review reviewers are launched concurrently (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:320-348`), so two backend starts/exits can overwrite each other's file contents. Once one pid record is lost, stale-run recovery will miss orphaned backend process groups and the new cleanup guarantee stops being reliable.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 25 -> Plan and Implement
